text-block-1.title: The Repository of All Truth
text-block-1.text:
The Java Content Repository (JCR) is the data layer that stores all content in Apache Sling. Think of it as the mainframe of the Matrix - the central repository where all information resides in a hierarchical tree structure.

Every piece of content, every component, every configuration is stored as nodes and properties in the JCR. Understanding this structure is key to mastering the Matrix.

code-block-1.title: JCR Node Structure Example
code-block-1.code: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <jcr:root xmlns:jcr="http://www.jcp.org/jcr/1.0" xmlns:nt="http://www.jcp.org/jcr/nt/1.0" xmlns:sling="http://sling.apache.org/jcr/sling/1.0" jcr:primaryType="nt:unstructured" jcr:title="The Matrix" sling:resourceType="sling-matrix/components/page"> <content jcr:primaryType="nt:unstructured" matrixColor="green" digitalRain="true" realityLevel="simulation"/> </jcr:root>

text-block-2.title: Core JCR Concepts
text-block-2.text:
• Nodes - Hierarchical containers for content (like folders)
  • Properties - Name-value pairs attached to nodes
  • Node Types - Define structure and allowed properties
  • Namespaces - Organize property and node type names
  • Workspaces - Separate content areas within the repository
